genre_refs: Charlotte Joan Sternberg folk-art painting ('Snowfall in the Village')
description: A reproduction of Charlotte Joan Sternberg's nostalgic folk-art winter painting 'Snowfall in the Village,' posted by the Department of Labor as a Sunday/holiday-season greeting. The painted (not designed-graphic) scene is a snowy New England town at dusk: a large decorated, lit Christmas tree stands at left in a snow-covered village green crisscrossed by roads; a horse-drawn sleigh and a horse-drawn carriage carry figures, children play and walk in red and dark winter coats, and a white-steepled church/clock-tower building and red colonial houses with glowing windows sit among bare trees and evergreens, with snow falling and rolling wooded hills behind. The palette is cool blues, whites, and warm window-light yellows with red accents. The artist's signature 'CHARLOTTE JOAN STERNBERG' is visible at lower left. There is no agency seal, slogan, or graphic overlay added; it is a straight reproduction of an existing artwork used as a seasonal message, so it is marked is_designed=false. The intent is a wholesome, patriotic-nostalgic greeting (the tweet adds 'May God continue to bless our great Nation and her People').
notable_text: (none added on image; artist signature 'Charlotte Joan Sternberg'; tweet titles it 'Snowfall in the Village')
